([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Aéroport international de Lomé-Tokoin*) ([IATA](/wiki/IATA_airport_code "IATA airport code"): **LFW**, [ICAO](/wiki/ICAO_airport_code "ICAO airport code"): **DXXX**), also known as **Gnassingbé Eyadéma International Airport** ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Aéroport international Gnassingbé Eyadéma*), is an [international airport](/wiki/International_airport "International airport") serving [Lomé](/wiki/Lom%C3%A9 "Lomé"), the capital of [Togo](/wiki/Togo "Togo"). [ASKY Airlines](/wiki/ASKY_Airlines "ASKY Airlines") has its [hub](/wiki/Airline_hub "Airline hub") at the airport. The airport is named after [Gnassingbé Eyadéma](/wiki/Gnassingb%C3%A9_Eyad%C3%A9ma "Gnassingbé Eyadéma"), the [third President](/wiki/List_of_presidents_of_Togo "List of presidents of Togo") of Togo.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Lomé-Tokoin Airport](/w/index.php?title=Lom%C3%A9-Tokoin_Airport&redirect=no "Lomé-Tokoin Airport"))

Airport in Lomé, Togo

**Lomé–Tokoin International Airport** ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Aéroport international de Lomé-Tokoin*) ([IATA](/wiki/IATA_airport_code "IATA airport code"): **LFW**, [ICAO](/wiki/ICAO_airport_code "ICAO airport code"): **DXXX**), also known as **Gnassingbé Eyadéma International Airport** ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Aéroport international Gnassingbé Eyadéma*), is an [international airport](/wiki/International_airport "International airport") serving [Lomé](/wiki/Lom%C3%A9 "Lomé"), the capital of [Togo](/wiki/Togo "Togo"). [ASKY Airlines](/wiki/ASKY_Airlines "ASKY Airlines") has its [hub](/wiki/Airline_hub "Airline hub") at the airport. The airport is named after [Gnassingbé Eyadéma](/wiki/Gnassingb%C3%A9_Eyad%C3%A9ma "Gnassingbé Eyadéma"), the [third President](/wiki/List_of_presidents_of_Togo "List of presidents of Togo") of Togo.

In 2014, the airport served 616,800 passengers. A new terminal at the airport opened in early 2016, with the capacity for up to 2 million passengers annually.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Accidents and incidents

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lom%C3%A9%E2%80%93Tokoin_International_Airport&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Accidents and incidents")]

* 26 December 1974: A [Grumman American G-1159 Gulfstream II](/wiki/Grumman_Gulfstream_II "Grumman Gulfstream II") (5V-TAA) of the Togolese government crashed on approach to Lome from [Niamey](/wiki/Niamey "Niamey"), killing 3 of the 6 occupants (the 3 crew were killed, but all 3 passengers survived). The plane was returning from a flight carrying Nigerien president [Seyni Kountche](/wiki/Seyni_Kountch%C3%A9 "Seyni Kountché") back to [Niger](/wiki/Niger "Niger"). The presidential jet was a replacement for a Douglas C-47 lost in January 1974.[[24]](#cite_note-24)
* 22 October 1977: a [Lockheed L-749A-79-52 Constellation](/wiki/Lockheed_Constellation "Lockheed Constellation") (N273R) of Lanzair, a stationary British cargo airline, was destroyed by fire.[[25]](#cite_note-25)
* 2 February 2008: a [Boeing 747-2D7B](/wiki/Boeing_747 "Boeing 747") (N527MC) on [Atlas Air](/wiki/Atlas_Air "Atlas Air") Flight 14 (Lome-[Amsterdam](/wiki/Amsterdam "Amsterdam")) had its cargo break loose during takeoff, breaking through the bulkhead and causing severe damage which led to the plane being written off.[[26]](#cite_note-26)
